##    5\. Drive-By For Beginners (with Natalie) 

But it's what happened AFTER the shoot that's the most amazing part to me...

![](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/k7xqmsLjTIOW2BVxKzdm_natalieConversation.png)

This was a great watch. I consider myself social and comfortable with interacting with strangers daily. BUT NOW I'm thinking of how i can push those limits even further. Or even How I can include them more in my comedy writing ,comedy creations and social media. Shout out to Natalie for putting herself out there and taking some great photos!

![Paulo Chikoti-Bandua](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/10cd8a9fb94eb43a1d9364db72f71061?d=mm&s=70)

Paulo Chikoti-Bandua

Awaiting Review 

5 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/8986816/comments/13724133)

Got lots of respect for Natalie.....I love the spontaneity of being able to get out of yourself, your head, your ego, your self-criticisms, and be in the moment with a complete stranger. I’m not a photographer but I’ve experienced similar “drive-by” activity for work, and even in church activities as a teen. This has me thinking about what that might look like with my poster art.

![Ashris Choudhury](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/4bc5e792ce4895a9603d9acf2dbb895e?d=mm)

5 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/8986816/comments/13730558)

Paulo, I swear... Drive-By has nothing to do with photography. It's all about seizing the moment and using the world around you to help you create your art. I'm excited to see you apply it to your poster art.

This is also what I needed to see to allow and be open to my friend shooting me for a video collab we are dreaming into life where I will be the dancer but I am camera shy but not so much when dancing in a stage but in front of the camera - a little eek, I want to dance for her and this but I freeze when it comes time to do it, trying to change and fix myself in that moment and ruin the moment. I needed this for so many reasons. Thank you.

![Tesz](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/53c8a56ae253476486259b645a6d0180?d=mm&s=70)

Tesz

Awaiting Review 

4 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/8986816/comments/15971751)

wow. This is so cool. I have goosebumps. First off, I love what she captures in her photos. She has a knack for capturing the moment when the person really looks into the camera and doesn't "see" the camera. It's like the person is looking at ME (the viewer) - it's awesome. She is really talented. Second, I love seeing her process of going through the nervousness and then doing it. I love the coaching. Telling her exactly what to look for and what to do. Golden. Thank you.

![Ashris Choudhury](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/4bc5e792ce4895a9603d9acf2dbb895e?d=mm)

Relating this to jumping out of my comfort zone with UX Design and job prospects... what Natalie had to say about removing yourself from your nerves and anxiety... observing rather than becoming consumed by it... I found this perspective something I could really employ in my own life

You're absolutely right. The whole book of dares is about applying the concept to what you do. Comfort zones are the killers of art.

![Gavin Wren](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/6e47838e1e80eb168bf3d03a56ce9f2f?d=mm&s=70)

Gavin Wren

Awaiting Review 

4 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/8986816/comments/15322629)

Trying to think of a way to adapt this. I worked as a food photographer for several years and did projects in the UK and latin America which required asking to take photos of strangers, their restaurants, shops etc but frankly I still never got comfortable with it. I originally practiced by walking up and down Oxford Street in London asking to take photos of people and doing the same at street food markets. Even though I've done it many, many times it still freaks me out slightly. Now my focus is creating video content for social media. Wondering whether to photo strangers again, simply because I know it's an uncomfortable exercise, or whether to add a different dimension such as interview, but to be honest, interviewing people is easier than photos-only.

![Ashris Choudhury](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/4bc5e792ce4895a9603d9acf2dbb895e?d=mm)

This was such a joy to watch! I've been trying to think of the musician's version of this challenge. Perhaps it could just be me asking random people if music is important to them, and if so, why. Then I could ask them to help me write a song on the spot! J/k...or am I???

![Father Bronques](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/6ab90ae46a200706e774b0b9fbeb0e8d?d=mm&s=70) Instructor 

Father Bronques

Awaiting Review 

5 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/8986816/comments/14828386)

PLAY YOUR MUSIC FOR STRANGERS CASEY! Listen to the keywords they use. Get a feel for what moves them about it; the sound? the lyrics? the drums? And use everything you learn in your marketing tools. Everything in The Book Of Dares is to help you prepare you for when the career defining moment is in front of you... And you'll be like "I got this".

![Leo Fialho](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/a6085461d9c8bb6dbd9ad4962b64222c?d=mm&s=70)

Leo Fialho

Awaiting Review 

5 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/8986816/comments/14795074)

This is very much like what we call “cold-scouting” in the Location Scouting business, where you walk up to someone’s house or business and ask them if you can photograph their property for consideration on a production. It’s nerve-wracking to start, and even though I have 17+ years experience I still get the anxiety when I start a cold-scouting day, but then you just do it once, get the ball rolling, and it all flows from there.

The secret of this whole section is precisely that, Leo. I'm preparing people for the cold-calling they're going to have to do later (Book Of Influence) if they want to take control of their careers.

YOOOO this is amazing. i really appreciate this video. WOULD LOVE LOVE to see more interactions on like this caught on video and hearing your feedback/advice to them. caught in real action . truly amazing. so awesome she was able to connect with him later! <3
